questions.stem: Which of the following best describes cultural relativism?

choices.text: An approach that encourages distinct cultural groups to keep their identities within one society.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The practice of understanding a culture by its own standards rather than judging it by another culture's.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A condition of normlessness in which social norms are weak, unclear, or conflicting.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Informal everyday norms whose violation brings mild disapproval rather than serious punishment.

questions.explanation: Cultural relativism is best described as the practice of understanding a culture by its own standards rather than judging it by another culture's. The other options describe different concepts.

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of problem-focused coping?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The initial surge of adrenaline and a racing heart when a threat first appears.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Making a study schedule to tackle the stress of an upcoming exam.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The long-term bodily toll of years of unrelieved stress.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Feeling fear and a pounding heart at the very same instant when facing danger.

questions.explanation: An example of problem-focused coping is: making a study schedule to tackle the stress of an upcoming exam. In general, problem-focused coping refers to coping by acting directly to address the source of a stressor. The other options illustrate different concepts.
